faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Teaneck home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small holes in wool, silk, or cashmere garments, webbing in closet corners, larvae casings on clothing or carpets, and adult moths flying around bedrooms or closets. You may also notice damage to upholstered furniture, area rugs, or stored blankets. If you notice any of these signs in your Teaneck home, contact us for a professional inspection.

faqs.question: Are clothes moth treatments safe for my family and pets in Teaneck?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products and application methods that are safe for your family and pets when applied by our trained professionals. We take extra precautions in homes with children, pets, and sensitive individuals, and provide clear instructions for any necessary preparation or re-entry times after treatment.
